title: 6 Managing the Electronic Bill Payments Database
body_text:
6	Managing the Electronic Bill Payments Database
6.1	Overview of the Electronic Bill Payments Database
The electronic bill payments database contains information such as the list of billers that each customer sets up, the customersâ biller account information, and the list of billers to which the customers have access.
Central 1 can hold your financial institutionâs electronic bill payments database. Alternatively, the financial institution or its banking service provider can create and manage the bill payments database.
6.2	The Financial Institution Manages the Electronic Bill Payments Database
If the financial institution or its banking service provider creates and manages the bill payments database, it is updated according to the financial institutionâs internal processes. Financial institutions that hold their own database can send bill payments to Central 1 via file transmission or direct connection.
To maintain biller information on the bill payments database, the financial institution obtains current biller information from Central 1. Financial institutions in Ontario can access e-Central to obtain this information (Services\Payments & Banking\e-Applications\e-Services\Bill Payment Vendor Information). Some financial institutions in Ontario use the Dynamic Link Library (DLL) to validate biller account numbers. The DLL is updated bi-weekly via the MerInfo file, which is stored on e-Central.
Financial institutions in other regions can download a text-only version of Central 1âs biller database via the File and Report Exchange, but they must contact Central 1 to request access. In addition, Central 1 publishes recent updates to biller information online. See Chapter 5, Viewing Biller Information.
Financial institutions can choose to validate bill payment information before it is sent to Central 1.
6.3	Central 1 Manages the Electronic Bill Payments Database
If the financial institutionâs bill payments database is held at Central 1, the financial institution updates the database by sending ARU messages via a direct connection to Central 1. These financial institutions can send bill payment transactions to Central 1 via direct connection or file transmission.
Central 1 updates biller information on the database, including biller account validation routines.
Exception
:	Central 1 canât hold the database for financial institutions that send batch files from Ontario. These financial institutions must create and manage their own database.
Central 1 will also remove inactive customer biller accounts daily in small batches. Customer biller accounts are considered inactive if they havenât been accessed within the past 26 months.
Important
:	This only affects the financial institutions for which Central 1 holds the bill payment biller account database. Customer impacts only occur if thereâs a need to apply a payment to a biller account thatâs been purged. The customer will need to set up the biller account again to make a payment to the specific biller.
END
